scams.title: Unlicensed taxi (illegal callvan)
scams.where: Hongdae, Itaewon, Gangnam after midnight when subway closes
scams.what: Black 'callvan' or unmarked car charges 3-4x metered taxi for ride. Especially at popular nightlife exits.
scams.avoid: Use Kakao T app (NOT Uber, weak in Korea). Wait at official taxi stands. Real taxis: silver/orange/black with TAXI roof sign + meter.

scams.title: Christian cult recruitment
scams.where: Hongdae, Sinchon, Gangnam streets — targets foreigners
scams.what: Friendly Korean approaches asking for directions, then invites to 'free Korean lesson' or 'cultural exchange'. Leads to multi-hour cult sessions, attempts at conversion + payment.
scams.avoid: Polite decline + walk on. Recognize patterns: clipboard, English skills mismatch, scripted questions about religion.

scams.title: Tourist-area restaurant overcharging
scams.what: Korean BBQ/seafood/ginseng chicken at 2-3x normal price; English menu has higher prices than Korean menu
scams.avoid: Use Naver Maps (Korean Yelp) reviews. Eat where Koreans eat — packed locals = good price + quality. Ask for Korean menu if available.

scams.title: Fashion district counterfeit
scams.what: Fake designer goods marketed as 'authentic', or knockoff K-beauty brands at inflated prices
scams.avoid: Designer = department stores (Lotte, Shinsegae, Hyundai). K-beauty: Olive Young (legit chain), Innisfree, Etude House official stores.

scams.title: ATM skimming + DCC
scams.what: Skimming + DCC trap (offer 'pay in your currency' — 5% worse rate)
scams.avoid: Use ATMs at major banks (KB, Shinhan, Woori) inside branches. Always select 'KRW' not USD on screen.

scams.title: Cosmetic surgery / clinic upselling
scams.what: $50 'consultation' becomes hard-sell pressure; deposit required for procedures pricier than promised
scams.avoid: Research clinics via Korea Tourism Org partner list. Get all prices in writing before deposit. Don't go in solo if cult-vulnerable.

scams.title: Aggressive selling in Insadong
scams.what: Vendors quote inflated prices for 'traditional Korean handicrafts' that are mass-produced
scams.avoid: Bargain (uncommon in Korea otherwise but expected here). Compare prices across multiple stalls.

scams.title: Free Wi-Fi phishing
scams.what: Fake Wi-Fi ('Korea Free' / 'Tourist Wi-Fi'); captures bank login data
scams.avoid: Use mobile data (eSIM/SIM) for banking. VPN if must use public Wi-Fi (NordVPN, ExpressVPN).

scams.title: DMZ tour bait-switch
scams.what: Cheap online listing turns out to be 'DMZ overview' from far away, no JSA visit
scams.avoid: Book via Koridoor, VIP Travel, or Klook with reviews. Confirm whether JSA (Joint Security Area) is included before paying.

scams.title: Karaoke (noraebang) overcharging
scams.what: Some 'karaoke' venues are room-salons with hostesses; charge $200-500 for drinks
scams.avoid: Use coin noraebang chains (multi-bang places) — fixed pricing. Or noraebang inside reputable buildings (not hidden alleys).
